name: terminal_ui_grok_v1_shell
purpose: Text-free full-screen operating-system shell for the OBE LAB terminal UI
prompt: A detailed smooth source illustration of an in-universe late-1980s Japanese PC-98 cyberpunk medical-laboratory computer interface, shown perfectly front-on as a full-screen operating environment with no physical monitor bezel. Oppressive clinical horror mood, deep petrol teal and cold cyan with restrained dark crimson warning accents, hard geometric light, harsh shadows, subtle analog CRT bloom and worn industrial texture. Composition designed for a 4:3 crop inside the landscape image: a narrow blank status bar across the top, an orderly left column of four unlabeled abstract application glyphs, one large EMPTY dark central workspace, a narrow blank footer strip, precise bevelled rectangular frames, inset wells, tiny indicator lamps, fine technical linework. Mature serious Snatcher and Policenauts-era industrial interface design, not cute, not playful. Keep every panel clean and spacious so custom game text can be added later. ABSOLUTELY NO WRITING OR TYPOGRAPHY ANYWHERE: no words, no letters, no numbers, no labels, no logos, no pseudo-text, no gibberish glyph rows, no signatures, no watermark. No people, no faces, no figures, no silhouettes, no hands, no characters. No icons containing letters. No Windows logo, no modern flat UI, no rounded smartphone cards. Smooth detailed source art, not blocky pixel art; runtime will apply PC-98 palette quantization and dithering. Symmetrical, orthographic, readable interface geometry, screen fills the frame.
